title: Child Neurology: Aicardi-Goutières Syndrome Presenting as Recurrent Ischemic Stroke.
abstract: Aicardi-Goutières syndrome (AGS) is a rare, single-gene disorder, characterized by neurologic and skin involvement with an increased level of interferon-α (IFN-α) in the CSF. We describe the case of a young patient presenting with recurrent ischemic stroke. Evaluation revealed the presence of chilblains, white matter abnormalities, cerebral atrophy, and raised IFN-α in the CSF. Compound heterozygous variants of
mesh_terms: Autoimmune Diseases of the Nervous System
